title: Highlight to Action
description: Perform actions on selected/highlighted text - translate, define, search, summarize, save, or share highlighted content.

title: Content Blocker
description: Block ads, trackers, popups, cookie notices, paywalls, or unwanted page elements.

title: Page Monitor
description: Monitor websites for changes, new content, price drops, or specific keywords and send alerts.

title: Rewards Engagement Assistant
description: High-level pattern for HR reward and recognition extensions. Supports daily reminders and contextual reward surfaces via content scripts.

title: Audio/Video Recording
description:
Record audio or video from browser tabs, meetings, or system audio.

⚠️ CRITICAL: This use case requires tabCapture and offscreen APIs for Manifest V3 compliance.

**Manifest V3 Restriction**: Service workers (background.js) CANNOT access navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia. You will get 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading getUserMedia)' error.

**MANDATORY ARCHITECTURE**:
1. **manifest.json**: Must include permissions: ['offscreen', 'tabCapture', 'tabs', 'activeTab']. The 'tabs' permission is required to check tab URLs before capture. The 'activeTab' permission allows temporary access to the active tab when user clicks the extension icon.
2. **offscreen.html**: Create a minimal HTML file to host the recording script.
3. **offscreen.js**: Handle navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia and MediaRecorder here. Convert recorded Blobs to Base64 strings (reader.readAsDataURL) and send Base64 data via chrome.runtime.sendMessage.
4. **background.js**: 
   - CRITICAL: Capture should ONLY be initiated when user explicitly clicks a button in the UI. This ensures the extension has been invoked on the page (activeTab requirement).
   - CRITICAL: You MUST validate the tab URL BEFORE calling chrome.tabCapture.getMediaStreamId. NEVER call getMediaStreamId without URL validation first.
   - Before calling chrome.tabCapture.getMediaStreamId, ALWAYS check the tab URL:
     - Get tab info: const tab = await chrome.tabs.get(tabId)
     - Check if URL is null/undefined - reject immediately
     - Check if URL starts with chrome://, chrome-extension://, edge://, about:, moz-extension://, opera://, brave:// - these CANNOT be captured, reject immediately
     - Check if URL is about:blank or empty string - reject immediately
     - Only proceed if URL starts with http:// or https://
     - Validate URL is a valid URL using new URL(tab.url)
     - Double-check URL one more time before calling getMediaStreamId (safety check)
   - If URL validation fails, send error message to UI and DO NOT call getMediaStreamId
   - Add a timeout (5 seconds) to chrome.tabCapture.getMediaStreamId to prevent hanging if extension wasn't invoked
   - Wrap getMediaStreamId in try-catch to handle capture errors
   - Get streamId using chrome.tabCapture.getMediaStreamId({ targetTabId: tabId }) ONLY after URL validation passes
   - Check if offscreen document exists before creating: await chrome.offscreen.hasDocument()
   - Create offscreen document if needed: await chrome.offscreen.createDocument({ url: 'offscreen.html', reasons: ['USER_MEDIA'], justification: 'Recording' })
   - Send streamId to offscreen document via chrome.runtime.sendMessage
   - Send status updates to UI during capture process (checking, requesting, success, error)
   - Proxy recording messages between offscreen and UI (sidepanel/popup)
   - Handle errors gracefully and notify UI if capture fails
5. **UI (sidepanel.js/popup.js)**: Receive Base64 string, convert back to Blob/Source for playback/download if needed, display user-friendly error messages if capture fails.

**ERROR HANDLING**:
- Always wrap chrome.tabCapture.getMediaStreamId in try-catch blocks.
- Add a timeout (Promise.race) to prevent hanging if getMediaStreamId never resolves.
- If capture fails, show user-friendly error messages.
- Always send status updates to UI so user knows what's happening (checking, requesting, success, error).
- Validate URL thoroughly before calling getMediaStreamId - check for chrome://, about:, and ensure it's http:// or https://.

⚠️ FAILURE TO FOLLOW THIS PATTERN WILL CAUSE THE EXTENSION TO FAIL.
